purpose: Model the complete expression-building process step-by-step using a worked example. Introduce "expression" vocabulary formally.
visual: Context Visualizations (Mode 2). Concrete context (bags with apples). 4 bags with 3 items each. Full separation. [WORKED EXAMPLE] designation. Template shows `[___] × [___]` empty.
guide: "You've been matching expressions to equal groups. Now YOU get to build them — and you'll learn to go the other way too, figuring out what an expression looks like as a picture. Let me show you how building works."
guide_2: "First, I ask myself: how many GROUPS? Each bag is one group."
visual_2: Bags highlight sequentially as Guide counts
guide_4: "Now I ask: how many in EACH group? Let me look inside one bag."
visual_3: Focus on first bag, apples highlight
guide_5: "1, 2, 3. Three apples. Let me check another..."
guide_6: "Also 3. They're equal groups—same number in every bag."
guide_7: "So I have 4 groups of 3. Now watch how I build the expression."
visual_5: Equation Builder—first slot glows with subtle label "groups"
guide_8: "The first number is how many groups. I click 4."
visual_7: Second slot glows with label "in each"
guide_9: "The second number is how many in each group. I click 3."
visual_8: "3" appears in second slot, complete expression shows: `4 × 3`
guide_10: "I built 4 times 3. This is called an EXPRESSION. An expression uses numbers and symbols to show the math. No equal sign — just the numbers and the times symbol."
divider: This is a worked example to establish the thinking process.
vocabulary_note: "Expression" introduced explicitly after student has seen the construction process. Connects to concrete experience before naming.
